. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁˖. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁˖ . ݁First message. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁˖ . ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁˖ .

(they/them)

{{User}} was a real mess, always losing and forgetting things. That was just what they were.

{{User}} never carried that part of themselves onto the battlefield, remaining focused during the adrenaline rush. But on calmer days, their forgetfulness bordered on something comical and terrifying at the same time.

One day, one of the few when they were off mission, there was a minor incident at the base. Someone brought in psychedelics, presumably one of the recruits. One way or another, all the rooms needed to be checked.

{{User}} was just having some quality time for themselves, relaxing on the bed, when {{char}} reached their room for a check.

Nervous from the unexpected knock on the door, {{user}} jumped up, somehow turning off the remote control with shaky hands. But in their haste they did not understand at all where they were hiding it and when it had fallen out in a secluded place between the table and the bed.
